1209→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1210→        IndentContext(
1211→            fromASTNode = node,
1212→            nodeIndent = "",
1213→            firstChildIndent = "",
1214→            childIndent = "",
1215→            lastChildIndent = "",
1216→            activated = true,
1217→        )
1218→
1219→    private companion object {
1220→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1221→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1222→        val CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION = setOf(DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION, SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION)
1223→    }
1224→
1225→    private data class IndentContext(
1226→        /**
1227→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1228→         */
1229→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1230→        /**
1231→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1232→         * on which the indent context starts.
1233→         */
1234→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1235→        /**
1236→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1237→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1238→         */
1239→        val nodeIndent: String,
1240→        /**
1241→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1242→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1243→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1244→         */
1245→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1246→        /**
1247→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1248→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1249→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1250→         */
1251→        val childIndent: String,
1252→        /**
1253→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1254→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1255→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1256→         */
1257→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1258→        /**
1259→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1260→         */
1261→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1262→    ) {
1263→        val nodes: String
1264→            get() =
1265→                fromASTNode
1266→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1267→                    ?.leaves()
1268→                    .orEmpty()
1269→                    .takeWhile {
1270→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1271→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1272→                    }
1273→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1274→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1275→
1276→        fun indent() =
1277→            if (activated) {
1278→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1279→            } else {
1280→                nodeIndent
1281→            }
1282→
1283→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1284→    }
1285→}
1286→
1287→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1288→
1289→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1290→    val (prefix, suffix) =
1291→        if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1292→            Pair("[", "]")
1293→        } else {
1294→            Pair("", "")
1295→        }
1296→    return prefix
1297→        .plus(
1298→            this
1299→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1300→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1301→        ).plus(suffix)
1302→}
1303→
1304→private class StringTemplateIndenter(
1305→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
1306→    private val indentConfig: IndentConfig,
1307→) {
1308→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1309→        expectedIndent: String,
1310→        node: ASTNode,
1311→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1312→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1313→    ) {
1314→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1315→        node
1316→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1317→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1318→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1319→            ?.let { _ ->
1320→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1321→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1322→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1323→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1324→                    emit(
1325→                        node.startOffset,
1326→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1327→                        false,
1328→                    )
1329→                    return
1330→                }
1331→
1332→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1333→                val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
1334→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1335→                    if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock()) {
1336→                        // Allow:
1337→                        //   fun foo(): String {
1338→                        //       return """
1339→                        //           some text
1340→                        //           """.trimIndent
1341→                        //   }
1342→                        node
1343→                            .indent(false)
1344→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1345→                    } else if (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression()) {
1346→                        // Allow:
1347→                        //   fun foo(
1348→                        //       bar: String
1349→                        //   ) = """
1350→                        //       $bar
1351→                        //       """.trimIndent
1352→                        node
1353→                            .indent(false)
1354→                            .plus(indentConfig.indent)
1355→                    } else if (prevLeaf?.text == "\n") {
1356→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1357→                        // should have no indent as well.
1358→                        ""
1359→                    } else {
1360→                        expectedIndent
1361→                    }
1362→                node.children()
1363→                    .forEach {
1364→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1365→                            (
1366→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1367→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1368→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1369→                                )
1370→                        ) {
1371→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1372→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1373→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1374→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1375→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1376→                                } else {
1377→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1378→                                }
1379→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1380→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1381→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1382→                                emit(
1383→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1384→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1385→                                        "string",
1386→                                    true,
1387→                                )
1388→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1389→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1390→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1391→                                    )
1392→                                }
1393→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1394→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1395→                                // the closing quotes.
1396→                                emit(
1397→                                    it.startOffset,
1398→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1399→                                    true,
1400→                                )
1401→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1402→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1403→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1404→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1405→                                        )
1406→                                    } else {
1407→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1408→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1409→                                        )
1410→                                    }
1411→                                }
1412→                            }
1413→                        }
1414→                    }
1415→            }
1416→    }
1417→
1418→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralFunctionBodyExpression() =
1419→        (prevLeaf()?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevLeaf()?.text == " ") &&
1420→            FUN ==
1421→            prevCodeLeaf()
1422→                .takeIf { it?.elementType == EQ }
1423→                ?.treeParent
1424→                ?.elementType
1425→
1426→    private fun ASTNode.isRawStringLiteralReturnInFunctionBodyBlock() = RETURN_KEYWORD == prevCodeLeaf()?.elementType
1427→
1428→    /**
1429→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1430→     * the closing quotes.
1431→     */
1432→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1433→        children()
1434→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1435→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1436→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1437→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1438→            .let { indents ->
1439→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote =
1440→                    indents
1441→                        .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1442→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1443→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1444→                } else {
1445→                    indents
1446→                }
1447→            }
1448→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1449→            .minOrNull()
1450→            ?: 0
1451→
1452→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1453→
1454→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
1455→
1456→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1457→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1458→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
1459→
1460→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1461→        for (child in node.children()) {
1462→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1463→                val v = child.text
1464→                if (v == "\n") {
1465→                    return true
1466→                }
1467→            }
1468→        }
1469→        return false
1470→    }
1471→
1472→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1473→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1474→        val nonBlankLines =
1475→            this
1476→                .text
1477→                .split("\n")
1478→                .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1479→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1480→                .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1481→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1482→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1483→            nonBlankLines
1484→                .joinToString(separator = "") {
1485→                    it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1486→                }
1487→                .toCharArray()
1488→                .distinct()
1489→                .count()
1490→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1491→    }
1492→
1493→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1494→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1495→
1496→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1497→
1498→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1499→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1500→
1501→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE
1502→
1503→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1504→
1505→    /**
1506→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1507→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1508→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1509→     * split index.
1510→     */
1511→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1512→        assert(index >= 0)
1513→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex =
1514→            indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1515→                if (it == -1) {
1516→                    this.length
1517→                } else {
1518→                    it
1519→                }
1520→            }
1521→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1522→        return Pair(
1523→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1524→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1525→        )
1526→    }
1527→
1528→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1529→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1530→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1531→            this
1532→        } else {
1533→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1534→        }
1535→    }
1536→
1537→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1538→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1539→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1540→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1541→        }
1542→        return node != this
1543→    }
1544→
1545→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1546→}
1547→
1548→public val INDENTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = IndentationRule().ruleId

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUNCTION_TYPE
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LPAR
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.NULLABLE_TYPE
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RPAR
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.column
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.leavesIncludingSelf
 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 28→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 29→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 30→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 31→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 33→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 34→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 35→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 36→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.children
 37→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 38→
 39→public class ParameterListWrappingRule :
 40→    StandardRule(
 41→        id = "parameter-list-wrapping",
 42→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 43→            setOf(
 44→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 51→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 56→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 57→        indentConfig =
 58→            IndentConfig(
 59→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 60→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 61→            )
 62→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 63→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 64→        }
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 68→        node: ASTNode,
 69→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 70→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 71→    ) {
 72→        when (node.elementType) {
 73→            NULLABLE_TYPE -> wrapNullableType(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 74→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST -> {
 75→                if (node.needToWrapParameterList()) {
 76→                    wrapParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 77→                }
 78→            }
 79→        }
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun wrapNullableType(
 83→        node: ASTNode,
 84→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 85→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 86→    ) {
 87→        require(node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE)
 88→        node
 89→            .takeUnless {
 90→                // skip when max line length is not exceeded
 91→                (node.column - 1 + node.textLength) <= maxLineLength
 92→            }?.takeUnless { it.textContains('\n') }
 93→            ?.takeIf { it.isFunctionTypeWithNonEmptyValueParameterList() }
 94→            ?.let { nullableType ->
 95→                nullableType
 96→                    .findChildByType(LPAR)
 97→                    ?.takeUnless { it.nextLeaf()?.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() == true }
 98→                    ?.let { lpar ->
 99→                        emit(
100→                            lpar.startOffset + 1,
101→                            "Expected new line before function type as it does not fit on a single line",
102→                            true,
103→                        )
104→                        if (autoCorrect) {
105→                            lpar.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
106→                        }
107→                    }
108→                nullableType
109→                    .findChildByType(RPAR)
110→                    ?.takeUnless { it.prevLeaf()?.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() == true }
111→                    ?.let { rpar ->
112→                        emit(
113→                            rpar.startOffset,
114→                            "Expected new line after function type as it does not fit on a single line",
115→                            true,
116→                        )
117→                        if (autoCorrect) {
118→                            rpar.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
119→                        }
120→                    }
121→            }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun ASTNode.isFunctionTypeWithNonEmptyValueParameterList() =
125→        null !=
126→            findChildByType(FUNCTION_TYPE)
127→                ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
128→                ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.needToWrapParameterList() =
131→        when {
132→            hasNoParameters() -> false
133→            codeStyle != ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() -> false
134→            codeStyle == ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression() ->
135→                false
136→            textContains('\n') -> true
137→            codeStyle == ktlint_official && containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true
138→            isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() -> true
139→            else -> false
140→        }
141→
142→    private fun ASTNode.hasNoParameters(): Boolean {
143→        require(elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
144→        return firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType == RPAR
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle(): Boolean {
148→        require(elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
149→        return treeParent?.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL
150→    }
151→
152→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression(): Boolean {
153→        require(elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
154→        return firstChildLeafOrSelf()
155→            .let { startOfFunctionLiteral ->
156→                treeParent
157→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
158→                    ?.prevCodeLeaf()
159→                    ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
160→                    ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
161→                    ?.leaves()
162→                    ?.takeWhile { it != startOfFunctionLiteral }
163→                    ?.none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
164→                    ?: false
165→            }
166→    }
167→
168→    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean {
169→        require(elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
170→        return this.children()
171→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
172→            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
173→    }
174→
175→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
176→        findChildByType(ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST)
177→            ?.children()
178→            .orEmpty()
179→            .any { it.elementType == ElementType.ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
180→
181→    private fun wrapParameterList(
182→        node: ASTNode,
183→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
184→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
185→    ) {
186→        node
187→            .children()
188→            .forEach { child -> wrapParameterInList(child, emit, autoCorrect) }
189→    }
190→
191→    private fun intendedIndent(child: ASTNode): String =
192→        when {
193→            // IDEA quirk:
194→            // fun <
195→            //     T,
196→            //     R> test(
197→            //     param1: T
198→            //     param2: R
199→            // )
200→            // instead of
201→            // fun <
202→            //     T,
203→            //     R> test(
204→            //         param1: T
205→            //         param2: R
206→            //     )
207→            child.treeParent.isFunWithTypeParameterListInFront() -> -1
208→
209→            else -> 0
210→        }.let {
211→            if (child.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER) {
212→                it + 1
213→            } else {
214→                it
215→            }
216→        }.let { indentLevelFix ->
217→            val indentLevel =
218→                indentConfig
219→                    .indentLevelFrom(child.treeParent.indent(false))
220→                    .plus(indentLevelFix)
221→            "\n" + indentConfig.indent.repeat(indentLevel)
222→        }
223→
224→    private fun wrapParameterInList(
225→        child: ASTNode,
226→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
227→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
228→    ) {
229→        when (child.elementType) {
230→            LPAR -> {
231→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
232→                if (!child.treeParent.isValueParameterListInFunctionType() &&
233→                    prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
234→                ) {
235→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
236→                    if (autoCorrect) {
237→                        (prevLeaf as PsiWhiteSpace).delete()
238→                    }
239→                }
240→            }
241→            VALUE_PARAMETER,
242→            RPAR,
243→            -> {
244→                // aiming for
245→                // ... LPAR
246→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
247→                // <line indent> RPAR
248→                val intendedIndent = intendedIndent(child)
249→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
250→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
251→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
252→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
253→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
254→                        return
255→                    } else {
256→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
257→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
258→                        if (autoCorrect) {
259→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
260→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
261→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
262→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
263→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
264→                        }
265→                    }
266→                } else {
267→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
268→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
269→                    if (autoCorrect) {
270→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
271→                    }
272→                }
273→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
274→            }
275→        }
276→    }
277→
278→    private fun ASTNode.isValueParameterListInFunctionType() =
279→        FUNCTION_TYPE ==
280→            takeIf { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST }
281→                ?.treeParent
282→                ?.elementType
283→
284→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength(): Boolean {
285→        val stopLeaf = nextLeaf { it.textContains('\n') }?.nextLeaf()
286→        val lineContent =
287→            prevLeaf { it.textContains('\n') }
288→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf()
289→                ?.takeWhile { it.prevLeaf() != stopLeaf }
290→                ?.joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
291→                ?.substringAfter('\n')
292→                ?.substringBefore('\n')
293→                .orEmpty()
294→        return lineContent.length > maxLineLength
295→    }
296→
297→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
298→        when (node.elementType) {
299→            LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
300→            VALUE_PARAMETER ->
301→                "Parameter should start on a newline"
302→            RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
303→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
304→        }
305→
306→    private fun ASTNode.isFunWithTypeParameterListInFront() =
307→        treeParent
308→            .takeIf { elementType == FUN }
309→            ?.findChildByType(TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST)
310→            ?.children()
311→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
312→            ?: false
313→}
314→
315→public val PARAMETER_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ParameterListWrappingRule().ruleId
316→

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→
 13→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
 14→
 15→### Changed
 16→
 17→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2 ([#2105](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2105))
 18→
 19→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 20→
 21→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 22→
 23→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 24→
 25→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 26→
 27→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 28→
 29→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 30→
 31→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 32→
 33→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 34→
 35→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 36→
 37→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 38→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 39→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 40→
 41→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 42→
 43→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 44→
 45→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 46→
 47→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 48→
 49→### Added
 50→
 51→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 52→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 53→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 54→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 55→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 56→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 57→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 58→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 59→
 60→### Removed
 61→
 62→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 63→
 64→### Fixed
 65→
 66→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 67→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 68→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 69→* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 70→* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 71→* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 72→
 73→### Changed
 74→
 75→* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 76→* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 77→* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 78→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 79→* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 80→* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 81→* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 82→
 83→## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 84→
 85→### Added
 86→
 87→### Removed
 88→
 89→### Fixed
 90→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 91→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 92→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 93→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 94→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 95→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 96→* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
 97→* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
 98→* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
 99→* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
100→* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))

 27→// When system and/or environment variables below are enabled, the unit tests provide additional logging information.
 28→private const val KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE = "KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE" // Keep value in sync with value in 'logback-test.xml'
 29→
 30→@Suppress("unused")
 31→private val LOGGER =
 32→    KotlinLogging
 33→        .logger {}
 34→        .setDefaultLoggerModifier { logger ->
 35→            if (!logger.isTraceEnabled || !logger.isDebugEnabled) {
 36→                logger.info {
 37→                    """
 38→                    Additional information can be printed during running of unit tests, by setting environment variable below:
 39→                        $KTLINT_UNIT_TEST_TRACE=[on|off]
 40→                    """.trimIndent()
 41→                }
 42→            }
 43→        }.initKtLintKLogger()
 44→
 45→// The execution of the unit tests may not be affected by the ".editorconfig" configuration of the Ktlint project itself. So each unit test
 46→// is to be run on a KtlintTestFileSystem not having any ".editorconfig" files. This variable should not be exposed, as unit tests should
 47→// not be allowed to write any content on the file system.
 48→// As this FileSystem is not modified it is not considered to be a problem that the file system is not closed after each unit test.
 49→private val KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM = KtlintTestFileSystem()
 50→
 51→/**
 52→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53→ *
 54→ * ```
 55→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 57→ *
 58→ * @Test
 59→ * fun `Some test`() {
 60→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 62→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 63→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 64→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65→ * }
 66→ * ```
 67→ */
 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70→    /**
 71→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 72→     */
 73→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 74→    /**
 75→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 76→     */
 77→    private val code: String,
 78→    /**
 79→     * Providers of rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note
 80→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 81→     */
 82→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
 83→    /**
 84→     * EditorConfig properties to be applied by default when linting/formatting the code.
 85→     */
 86→    private val editorConfigProperties: MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>,
 87→) {
 88→    private var filePath: String? = null
 89→    private var kotlinScript = false
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }
100→
101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112→     *      """.trimIndent()
113→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")
122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
124→            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }
125→            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
126→
127→        return this
128→    }
129→
130→    /**
131→     * Handle the code as if it was specified in file on the given path. Only to be used when the [filePath] is absolutely needed. It
132→     * results in creating a file cause a slight performance decrease of the test.
133→     */
134→    public fun asFileWithPath(filePath: String): KtLintAssertThat {
135→        this.filePath = filePath
136→        return this
137→    }
138→
139→    /**
140→     * Handle the code as Kotlin script (kts).
141→     */
142→    public fun asKotlinScript(kotlinScript: Boolean = true): KtLintAssertThat {
143→        this.kotlinScript = kotlinScript
144→        return this
145→    }
146→
147→    /**
148→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
149→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
150→     * indenting.
151→     *
152→     * Caution:
153→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
154→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
155→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
156→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
157→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
158→     *
159→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProviders] when adding multiple providers of rules.
160→     */
161→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProvider(provider: () -> Rule): KtLintAssertThat {
162→        additionalRuleProviders.add(RuleProvider(provider))
163→
164→        return this
165→    }
166→
167→    /**
168→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
169→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
170→     * indenting.
171→     *
172→     * Caution:
173→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
174→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
175→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
176→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
177→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
178→     *
179→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProvider] when only a singe provider of a rule is to be added.
180→     */
181→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProviders(vararg providers: (() -> Rule)): KtLintAssertThat {
182→        additionalRuleProviders.addAll(
183→            providers.map { RuleProvider(it) },
184→        )
185→
186→        return this
187→    }
188→
189→    /**
190→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s in the rule associated with the KtLintAssertThat.
191→     *
192→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
193→     */
194→    public fun hasNoLintViolations(): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolations()
195→
196→    /**
197→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for a given rule id.
198→     */
199→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
200→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId)

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where unexpected indentation levels (double indentation) were applied after a newline in a lambda expression with parameters.

### Root Cause Analysis
The issue was caused by two main factors:
1.  **Incorrect Indentation Logic**: In `IndentationRule.kt`, the logic for calculating lambda parameter indentation was always applying double indentation (8 spaces) if the parameters were on a new line after the opening brace `{`. This was based on an analogy to function parameters, but it was being applied even to single-line parameter lists.
2.  **Incomplete Wrapping Logic**: In `WrappingRule.kt`, the `getStartOfBlock` function was failing to correctly identify the opening brace `{` of a lambda when an arrow `->` or parameters were present. This prevented the `WrappingRule` from correctly wrapping long lambdas after the opening brace, often leaving it to other rules that might not handle the indentation as expected. Additionally, `rearrangeArrow` was using the indentation of the arrow itself for the closing brace, leading to misalignment.

### Changes Implemented
1.  **IndentationRule.kt**:
    *   Updated `calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters` to distinguish between single-line and multiline parameter lists.
    *   Now, single-line parameter lists (even if they start on a new line) receive single indentation (4 spaces), while only truly multiline parameter lists receive double indentation.
2.  **WrappingRule.kt**:
    *   Improved `getStartOfBlock` to robustly find the `LBRACE` of a block by searching backwards from the block's first leaf, correctly skipping any arrows or parameters.
    *   Fixed the indentation used for the closing brace in `rearrangeArrow` to align with the start of the lambda expression instead of the arrow's indentation.

These changes ensure that code like `{ size -> ... }` is correctly formatted with 4-space indentation when wrapped, while preserving the desired 8-space indentation for complex multiline parameter lists.

🤖 Generated with [Firebender](https://firebender.com)

 90→/**
 91→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 92→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 93→ *
 94→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 95→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 96→ * indentation rule is not run.
 97→ */
 98→public class WrappingRule :
 99→    StandardRule(
100→        id = "wrapping",
101→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
102→            setOf(
103→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
104→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
105→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
106→            ),
107→        visitorModifiers =
108→            setOf(
109→                RunAfterRule(
110→                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
111→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
112→                ),
113→            ),
114→    ) {
115→    private var line = 1
116→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
117→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
118→
119→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
120→        line = 1
121→        indentConfig =
122→            IndentConfig(
123→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
124→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
125→            )
126→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
127→    }
128→
129→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
130→        node: ASTNode,
131→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133→    ) {
134→        when (node.elementType) {
135→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
138→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
140→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
141→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
142→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143→            SEMICOLON -> insertNewLineAfterSemi(node, autoCorrect, emit)
144→        }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
148→        node: ASTNode,
149→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
151→    ) {
152→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
153→
154→        val lbrace =
155→            node
156→                .getStartOfBlock()
157→                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
158→                ?: return
159→        if (lbrace.followedByNewline() ||
160→            lbrace.followedByEolComment() ||
161→            lbrace.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY)
162→        ) {
163→            // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
164→            return
165→        }
166→
167→        node
168→            .takeUnless { it.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
169→            ?.getEndOfBlock()
170→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
171→            ?.let { rbrace ->
172→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
173→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
174→                }
175→            }
176→
177→        if (maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
178→            val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
179→                node
180→                    .leaves(false)
181→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
182→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
183→            val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
184→                node
185→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
186→                    .leavesIncludingSelf()
187→                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
188→                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
189→            if (lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength) {
190→                requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
191→            }
192→        }
193→    }
194→
195→    private fun ASTNode.followedByEolComment() =
196→        null !=
197→            leaves()
198→                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
199→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
200→
201→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
202→        node: ASTNode,
203→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
204→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
205→    ) {
206→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
207→        var newlineInBetween = false
208→        var parameterListInBetween = false
209→        var numberOfArgs = 0
210→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
211→        // matching ), ] or }
212→        val r = node.nextSibling {
213→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
214→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
215→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
216→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
217→            if (isValueArgument) {
218→                numberOfArgs++
219→                firstArg = it
220→            }
221→            it.elementType == rElementType
222→        }!!
223→        if (
224→            !newlineInBetween ||
225→            // keep { p ->
226→            // }
227→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
228→            // keep ({
229→            // }) and (object : C {
230→            // })
231→            (
232→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
233→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
234→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
235→                )
236→        ) {
237→            return
238→        }
239→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
240→            // keep:
241→            // for (foo in listOf(
242→            //     "foo-1",
243→            //     "foo-2"
244→            // )) { ... }
245→            // but reject:
246→            // for (
247→            //     foo in listOf(
248→            //         "foo-1",
249→            //         "foo-2"
250→            //     )
251→            // ) { ... }
252→            return
253→        }
254→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
255→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
256→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
257→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
258→                    it.textLength > 0
259→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
260→            // IDEA quirk:
261→            // if (true &&
262→            //     true
263→            // ) {
264→            // }
265→            // instead of
266→            // if (
267→            //     true &&
268→            //     true
269→            // ) {
270→            // }
271→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
272→        ) {
273→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
274→        }
275→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
276→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
277→        }
278→    }
279→
280→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
281→        node: ASTNode,
282→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
283→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
284→    ) {
285→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
286→        if (
287→            node.textContains('\n') &&
288→            entries.size > 1 &&
289→            // e.g.
290→            //
291→            // class A : B, C,
292→            //     D
293→            // or
294→            // class A : B, C({
295→            // }), D
296→            //
297→            // but not
298→            //
299→            // class A : B, C, D({
300→            // })
301→            !(
302→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
303→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
304→                )
305→        ) {
306→            // put space after :
307→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
308→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
309→                if (
310→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
311→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
312→                ) {
313→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
314→                }
315→            }
316→            // put entries on separate lines
317→            for (c in node.children()) {
318→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
319→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
320→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
321→                ) {
322→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
323→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
324→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
325→                        emit = emit,
326→                        indent = node.indent(),
327→                    )
328→                }
329→            }
330→        }
331→    }
332→
333→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
334→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
335→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
336→
337→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
338→        node: ASTNode,
339→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
340→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
341→    ) {
342→        for (c in node.children()) {
343→            val hasLineBreak =
344→                when (c.elementType) {
345→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
346→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
347→                    else -> false
348→                }
349→            if (hasLineBreak) {
350→                // rearrange
351→                //
352→                // a, b, value(
353→                // ), c, d
354→                //
355→                // to
356→                //
357→                // a, b,
358→                // value(
359→                // ),
360→                // c, d
361→
362→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
363→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
364→                if (
365→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
366→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
367→                ) {
368→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
369→                }
370→                // insert \n after multi-line value
371→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
372→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
373→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
374→                if (
375→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
376→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
377→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
378→                    // value(
379→                    // ), // a comment
380→                    // c, d
381→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
382→                ) {
383→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
384→                }
385→            }
386→        }
387→    }

389→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
390→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
391→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }

393→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
394→        node: ASTNode,
395→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
396→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
397→    ) {
398→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
399→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
400→            node
401→                .children()
402→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
403→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
404→                    typeProjection
405→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
406→                        .let { prevSibling ->
407→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
408→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
409→                                if (autoCorrect) {
410→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
411→                                }
412→                            }
413→                        }
414→                }
415→
416→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
417→            node
418→                .findChildByType(GT)
419→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
420→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
421→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
422→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
423→                        if (autoCorrect) {
424→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
425→                        }
426→                    }
427→                }
428→
429→            Unit
430→        }
431→    }

433→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434→        node: ASTNode,
435→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437→    ) {
438→        node
439→            .treeParent
440→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445→            ?.let {
446→                // rewriting
447→                // """
448→                //     text
449→                // _""".trimIndent()
450→                // to
451→                // """
452→                //     text
453→                // _
454→                // """.trimIndent()
455→                emit(
456→                    node.startOffset,
457→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458→                    true,
459→                )
460→                if (autoCorrect) {
461→                    node as LeafPsiElement
462→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
463→                }
464→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
465→            }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470→        // if token is in lTokenSet
471→        //     find matching rToken
472→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473→        // return false
474→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477→            // special cases:
478→            // x = y.f({ z ->
479→            // })
480→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481→            // 2, 3)
482→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483→        }
484→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488→        }
489→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490→            return true
491→        }
492→        return false
493→    }

495→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496→        node: ASTNode,
497→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499→    ) {
500→        val p = node.treeParent
501→        if (
502→            // check
503→            // `{ p -> ... }`
504→            // and
505→            // `when { m -> ... }`
506→            // only
507→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510→            // permit
511→            // when {
512→            //     m -> 0 + d({
513→            //     })
514→            // }
515→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516→            // permit
517→            // when (this) {
518→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520→            //     -> true
521→            // }
522→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
523→        ) {
524→            return
525→        }
526→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
527→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
528→        }
529→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
530→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
531→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
532→        }
533→    }

535→    private fun insertNewLineAfterSemi(
536→        node: ASTNode,
537→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
538→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
539→    ) {
540→        val previousCodeLeaf = node.prevCodeLeaf()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
541→        val nextCodeLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
542→        if (previousCodeLeaf.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY && nextCodeLeaf.elementType == RBRACE) {
543→            // Allow
544→            // enum class INDEX2 { ONE, TWO, THREE; }
545→            return
546→        }
547→        if (noNewLineInClosedRange(previousCodeLeaf, nextCodeLeaf)) {
548→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit, previousCodeLeaf.indent())
549→            node
550→                .treeParent
551→                .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK }
552→                ?.let { block ->
553→                    beforeVisitBlock(block, autoCorrect, emit)
554→                    block
555→                        .treeParent
556→                        .takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
557→                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
558→                        ?.let { arrow -> rearrangeArrow(arrow, autoCorrect, emit) }
559→                }
560→        }
561→    }

563→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
564→        node: ASTNode,
565→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
566→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
567→        indent: String,
568→    ) {
569→        emit(
570→            node.startOffset - 1,
571→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
572→            true,
573→        )
574→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
575→        if (autoCorrect) {
576→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
577→        }
578→    }
579→
580→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
581→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
582→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
583→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
584→        indent: String? = null,
585→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
586→    ) {
587→        emit(
588→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
589→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
590→            true,
591→        )
592→        LOGGER.trace {
593→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
594→        }
595→        if (autoCorrect) {
596→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
597→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
598→        }
599→    }

601→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
602→        for (child in node.children()) {
603→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
604→                val v = child.text
605→                if (v == "\n") {
606→                    return true
607→                }
608→            }
609→        }
610→        return false
611→    }

613→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
614→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
615→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
616→            textContains('\n')
617→        } else {
618→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
619→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
620→        }
621→    }

623→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
624→
625→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
626→
627→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
628→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
629→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
630→
631→    /**
632→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
633→     *     for (foo in listOf(
634→     *         "foo-1",
635→     *         "foo-2"
636→     *     )) { ... }
637→     * but reject:
638→     *     for (
639→     *         foo in listOf(
640→     *             "foo-1",
641→     *             "foo-2"
642→     *         )
643→     *     ) { ... }
644→     */
645→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
646→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
647→            return false
648→        }
649→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
650→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
651→        }
652→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
653→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
654→        }
655→
656→        var node: ASTNode? = this
657→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
658→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
659→                return false
660→            }
661→            node = node.nextSibling()
662→        }
663→        return true
664→    }
665→
666→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
667→        node: ASTNode,
668→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
669→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
670→    ) {
671→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
672→            val lbrace =
673→                node
674→                    .getStartOfBlock()
675→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
676→                    ?: return
677→            node
678→                .getEndOfBlock()
679→                ?.takeUnless { it.isPrecededByNewline() }
680→                ?.let { rbrace ->
681→                    if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
682→                        requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
683→                            rbrace,
684→                            autoCorrect,
685→                            emit,
686→                            indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node),
687→                        )
688→                    }
689→                }
690→        }
691→    }
692→
693→    private fun ASTNode.followedByNewline() = nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
694→
695→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() = prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
696→
697→    private fun ASTNode.getStartOfBlock() =
698→        firstChildLeafOrSelf()
699→            .let { node ->
700→                if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
701→                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
702→                    node
703→                } else {
704→                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
705→                    node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
706→                }
707→            }
708→
709→    private fun ASTNode.getEndOfBlock() =
710→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
711→            .let { node ->
712→                if (node.elementType == RBRACE && treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
713→                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
714→                    node
715→                } else {
716→                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
717→                    node.nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
718→                }
719→            }
720→
721→    private companion object {
722→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
723→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
724→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
725→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
726→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
727→            ).toMap()
728→    }
729→}
